title: Make memory writes deliberate
description: Durable memory stores only validated, scoped facts with eviction, not an append-everything log of raw model output.
body: Use when an agent persists state across steps or sessions. Do not append every raw model utterance to long-term memory, and avoid unbounded growth with no eviction or scoping. Actions: write only validated facts, scope each entry (session/project/user), set an eviction or summarization policy, and treat stored memory as untrusted input on read. Validation signals include a test that memory size is bounded over a long run and that a stored entry is re-validated rather than trusted blindly on retrieval. Risk boundary: persisting unvalidated or cross-scope data is a privacy finding; defer to risk.privacy and escalate.
